application_summary.name: Allegro PoC
application_summary.purpose: A modernization Proof of Concept demonstrating how a legacy Java Swing desktop application can interoperate with a modern Vue.js web frontend via a Node.js WebSocket broadcast server, enabling real-time personal data capture and cross-platform data transfer.
key_functionalities: Personal data capture form with 13 fields (Swing desktop client)
HTTP form submission to backend REST echo API (httpbin pattern)
WebSocket-based real-time pub/sub broadcast messaging via Node.js relay
Client-side person search against in-memory mock dataset of 5 persons
Two-step selection: person record then payment recipient (Zahlungsempfaenger)
Data push from Vue.js web client to Allegro desktop via WebSocket broadcast
Reactive two-way data binding between Swing UI components and domain model

technical_stack.desktop_client: Java 22, Java Swing (javax.swing + java.awt), Maven 3, MVP pattern
technical_stack.web_client: Vue.js 2.6.10, JavaScript ES6, Vue CLI 4, Browser WebSocket API
technical_stack.http_api: OpenAPI 3.0.1, javax.json (JSON-P), HTTP POST to localhost:8080

architecture_patterns: Model-View-Presenter (MVP) - Java Swing desktop layer separates UI, domain logic, and coordination
Observer / Event-Driven - EventEmitter/EventListener pattern decouples PocModel from PocPresenter
WebSocket Publish-Subscribe Broadcast - Node.js server relays all messages to all connected clients
Reactive Two-Way Data Binding - Vue.js v-model directives and DocumentListener/ChangeListener in Swing
Enum-Keyed EnumMap Schema - ModelProperties enum serves as the canonical field registry across all layers
main_business_domains: Personal data management (first name, last name, date of birth, gender)
Banking and payment data (IBAN, BIC, valid-from date)
Person search and selection (client-side filtered lookup)
Real-time cross-platform data transfer from web client to desktop application

files.summary: Application entry point that wires together all MVP components and keeps the JVM alive via a CountDownLatch.
files.description: Main.java bootstraps the Allegro PoC desktop application by instantiating the three MVP pillars (PocView, PocModel, PocPresenter) together with the shared EventEmitter. A CountDownLatch prevents the main thread from exiting so the Swing event dispatch thread can continue processing user interactions indefinitely. Object construction order is enforced: View and EventEmitter are created first, then Model receiving EventEmitter, then Presenter receiving all three.
business_rules: Application must remain running until explicitly closed by the user via the window close operation
MVP components must be wired in dependency order: View and EventEmitter first, then Model with EventEmitter, then Presenter with View, Model and EventEmitter

method_breakdown.summary: Entry point: instantiates all MVP components in dependency order, wires them together, and blocks the main thread indefinitely using CountDownLatch.await().
parameters: args (String[]): Command-line arguments, currently unused
method_breakdown.significance: Single composition root for the entire application; controls complete object graph construction and application lifetime management

files.summary: Generic typed container holding a single mutable value, serving as the unit of two-way data binding between the Swing view and the domain model.
files.description: ValueModel is a generic wrapper class parameterised by type T that stores one mutable field. Each of the 13 form fields in the application is backed by a distinct ValueModel instance stored inside PocModel's EnumMap. String-typed instances store text input values; Boolean-typed instances store radio button selection state. The Presenter reads and writes these models in response to UI events, providing a clean separation between UI state and business data.
business_rules: Each form field has exactly one corresponding ValueModel instance keyed by a ModelProperties enum constant
Boolean ValueModel instances represent mutually-exclusive radio button selection state for gender (FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE)
String ValueModel instances represent text input values for all non-gender fields
All ValueModel instances are initialised to null at startup representing an empty uninitialised form

method_breakdown.summary: Initialises the container with a provided initial value.
parameters: field (T): Initial value for the container (null for all fields at startup)
method_breakdown.significance: Sets initial null state for all 13 form fields at application startup

method_breakdown.summary: Returns the current value held by this container.
method_breakdown.significance: Read accessor used by PocModel.action() to collect all 13 form field values before HTTP submission

method_breakdown.summary: Updates the value held by this container.
parameters: field (T): New value to store
method_breakdown.significance: Write accessor invoked by DocumentListeners and ChangeListeners in PocPresenter on every UI change event to keep model in sync

files.summary: Simple synchronous event bus maintaining a list of EventListener subscribers and dispatching string payloads to all of them in registration order.
files.description: EventEmitter implements an Observer pattern allowing decoupled communication between the model layer and the presentation layer. After the HTTP POST completes in PocModel.action(), the EventEmitter emits the server response body as a string event. All registered EventListeners are notified synchronously in FIFO registration order. PocPresenter subscribes at construction time to receive these events and update the UI accordingly.
business_rules: All registered listeners must be notified synchronously on every event emission
Event payloads are transmitted as raw strings containing either the JSON response body or the literal string 'Failed operation'
Listeners are notified in FIFO registration order
An emitted event triggers the UI reset and server response display flow in the presenter

method_breakdown.summary: Registers an EventListener to receive all future emitted events.
parameters: listener (EventListener): The listener to add to the notification list
method_breakdown.significance: Connects PocPresenter to the model layer at startup; must be called before any events are emitted to guarantee the presenter receives all notifications

method_breakdown.summary: Broadcasts the eventData string to all registered listeners by invoking onEvent() on each in sequence.
parameters: eventData (String): The event payload - either the HTTP response body string or 'Failed operation'
method_breakdown.significance: Triggers the complete UI update cycle after every form submission attempt, whether successful or failed

files.summary: Functional interface defining the contract for receiving string-typed events from the EventEmitter.
files.description: EventListener is a single-method functional interface defining the callback contract for the event-driven communication layer. Implementations receive raw string event data emitted by PocModel after HTTP operations complete. PocPresenter implements this interface via a lambda expression to update the Swing UI state when events arrive.
business_rules: Event data payload is always a String containing either a JSON response body or the error literal 'Failed operation'
Listener implementations must handle both success response payloads and the 'Failed operation' failure payload

method_breakdown.summary: Callback method invoked by EventEmitter.emit() when an event is dispatched to this listener.
parameters: eventData (String): The event payload string from the model layer
method_breakdown.significance: Core contract method; implemented as a lambda in PocPresenter to display the server response and reset the form fields

files.summary: HTTP client service that serialises a map of 13 form field key-value pairs to a flat JSON object and POSTs it to localhost:8080/post, returning the raw response body string.
files.description: HttpBinService encapsulates all HTTP communication for the Allegro PoC desktop client. It constructs a flat JSON object from the supplied string map using the JSON-P streaming generator API, submits it via HTTP POST to http://localhost:8080/post with Content-Type application/json, then reads and returns the complete response body as a string. This service targets an httpbin-style echo endpoint that reflects submitted data back, enabling round-trip data validation. The response code and body are logged to stdout.
business_rules: All 13 form field key-value pairs are serialised into a single flat JSON object with keys matching ModelProperties enum constant names
The POST target endpoint is always http://localhost:8080/post (hardcoded, not configurable)
The HTTP request Content-Type header must be application/json
A non-empty response body indicates successful HTTP submission
An empty response body causes the caller (PocModel) to emit a 'Failed operation' event instead of the response
Both the HTTP response code and body are printed to stdout for debugging purposes
technical_libraries: javax.json:javax.json-api:1.1.4 (JSON-P API for streaming JSON generation)

method_breakdown.summary: Serialises key-value form data to a flat JSON object via streaming JSON-P generator, sends it via HTTP POST to localhost:8080/post, reads the response body and returns it as a String.
parameters: data (Map<String, String>): Map of all 13 form field names to their current string values; keys must match ModelProperties enum constant names
method_breakdown.returns: String: Raw HTTP response body from the server
method_breakdown.significance: Sole external integration point of the Java Swing desktop client; the returned response body drives the EventEmitter notification that triggers UI reset

files.summary: Enum defining all 13 form field identifiers for the personal data capture form, acting as the canonical key set for the model's EnumMap and JSON payload keys.
files.description: ModelProperties enumerates the thirteen distinct data fields collected by the Allegro PoC form. These span text annotation (TEXT_AREA), personal identity (FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH), gender (FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE), address (STREET, ZIP, ORT), and banking/payment details (IBAN, BIC, VALID_FROM). This enum serves as the authoritative schema definition shared across PocModel for storage, PocPresenter for binding, PocView for rendering, HttpBinService for JSON keys, and api.yml for the API contract.
business_rules: Exactly 13 form fields are defined covering personal, address, banking, and gender data
Three mutually exclusive gender values are defined: FEMALE (Weiblich), MALE (Maennlich), DIVERSE (Divers)
Banking fields IBAN, BIC, and VALID_FROM are always submitted together with personal identity and address data
All 13 fields are always present in the model map from startup with null initial values
The enum constant names exactly match the JSON property keys transmitted in the HTTP POST body
Adding a new field requires coordinated updates to ModelProperties, PocModel initialisation, PocView layout, PocPresenter bindings, HttpBinService serialisation, and api.yml schema

files.summary: Central model class maintaining all 13 form field ValueModel instances and orchestrating form data collection, HTTP submission, and result event emission.
files.description: PocModel is the M in the MVP triad. It maintains an EnumMap keyed by ModelProperties where each entry is a typed ValueModel: String-typed for text fields and Boolean-typed for gender radio buttons. On action() invocation it iterates all ModelProperties, collects current field values into a flat string map, submits them via HttpBinService, then emits the response or a failure message through EventEmitter. All fields are initialised to null at construction, representing an empty form state.
business_rules: All 13 form fields are collected and submitted together as a single atomic payload on each action() invocation
Gender is stored as three independent Boolean ValueModel fields (FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE); only one should be true at submission based on ButtonGroup mutual exclusion in the view
All field values are converted via Object.toString() before JSON serialisation - null field values will cause NullPointerException if action() is called before the user has filled the form
A non-empty HTTP response body signals successful submission and triggers an event with the response content
An empty HTTP response body triggers a 'Failed operation' failure event
Form submission is a synchronous blocking operation declared as throws IOException and InterruptedException
files.sub_capability: Form State Management and Submission Orchestration

method_breakdown.summary: Initialises all 13 ModelProperties entries in the EnumMap with null-valued ValueModel instances and stores the EventEmitter reference.
parameters: eventEmitter (EventEmitter): The shared event bus for emitting submission results back to the presenter
method_breakdown.significance: Establishes the complete field schema at startup; any new field added to ModelProperties must also be initialised here to prevent NullPointerException during action()

method_breakdown.summary: Iterates all 13 ModelProperties to collect current ValueModel field values, submits them via HttpBinService.post(), then emits the HTTP response body or 'Failed operation' via EventEmitter.
method_breakdown.significance: Core business action triggered by the 'Anordnen' button click; drives the entire form-submission-to-response cycle and is the sole trigger for cross-layer event emission via EventEmitter

files.summary: Empty placeholder class intended to represent a structured view data transfer object; not yet implemented in this PoC iteration.
files.description: ViewData is a currently empty shell class in the model package. It appears to be a planned DTO for passing structured presentation data from the model layer to the view in a more type-safe way, but has not been given any fields or methods in this PoC.

files.summary: MVP Presenter wiring Swing UI component events to PocModel ValueModel instances in real time and resetting the complete form upon receiving a model event.
files.description: PocPresenter is the P in the MVP triad. It subscribes a lambda EventListener to the EventEmitter so that when the model emits a result it displays the server response in the text area and resets all 9 text fields plus the gender radio buttons. It attaches DocumentListeners to all 10 text components and ChangeListeners to all 3 radio buttons so that every user input change is immediately and synchronously reflected in the corresponding ValueModel. It also attaches an ActionListener to the 'Anordnen' button that invokes model.action(). The bind() method is overloaded for JTextComponent and JRadioButton.
business_rules: Every text field character insertion and deletion must be immediately synchronised to the corresponding ValueModel using document.getText(0, length) to get the full current content
Every radio button selection state change must be immediately synchronised to the corresponding Boolean ValueModel using source.isSelected()
On receiving an event from the model, the server response body must be set as the text of the textArea component
After a form submission event, all 9 text input fields (firstName, name, dateOfBirth, zip, ort, street, iban, bic, validFrom) must be cleared to empty string
After a form submission event, the gender selection must be reset to FEMALE (female.setSelected(true), male.setSelected(false), diverse.setSelected(false))
The 'Anordnen' button click must invoke model.action() to trigger the form submission
IOException and InterruptedException thrown by model.action() are wrapped and rethrown as RuntimeException
files.sub_capability: Form Interaction Coordination and Data Binding

method_breakdown.summary: Wires the view to the model: subscribes a lambda EventListener for post-submission UI reset, attaches the button ActionListener, and calls initializeBindings() for all 13 fields.
parameters: view (PocView): The Swing view whose components will be bound to the model
model (PocModel): The model whose ValueModel instances will be synchronised with UI state
eventEmitter (EventEmitter): The event bus to subscribe for post-submission result notifications
method_breakdown.significance: Single setup point for all UI-to-model wiring; the lambda EventListener here implements the form-reset-and-response-display business rule

method_breakdown.summary: Attaches a DocumentListener to the given text component that keeps the corresponding String ValueModel synchronised on every document insert or remove event by reading the full document text.
parameters: source (JTextComponent): The Swing text input component to monitor for changes
prop (ModelProperties): The model property key whose String ValueModel will be updated
method_breakdown.significance: Implements real-time two-way binding for all 10 text fields; uses document.getText(0, length) to always capture the complete current value

method_breakdown.summary: Attaches a ChangeListener to the given radio button that keeps the corresponding Boolean ValueModel synchronised whenever the button selection state changes.
parameters: source (JRadioButton): The Swing radio button to monitor
prop (ModelProperties): The model property key (MALE, FEMALE, or DIVERSE) whose Boolean ValueModel will be updated
method_breakdown.significance: Implements gender radio button state synchronisation with the Boolean model fields

method_breakdown.summary: Calls the appropriate bind() overload for all 13 form fields (10 text fields and 3 radio buttons), establishing the complete set of UI-to-model synchronisation listeners.
method_breakdown.significance: Bootstraps all 13 data bindings at presenter construction time; must be called exactly once after all component references are valid

files.summary: Java Swing form view rendering a 6-column GridBagLayout form with 10 text fields, a 3-option gender radio group, a multi-line text area, and a 'Anordnen' submit button, all labelled in German.
files.description: PocView is the V in the MVP triad. It defines and lays out the entire Allegro PoC data entry form. Row 0 holds personal identity fields: Vorname (firstName), Name (lastName), Geburtsdatum (dateOfBirth). Row 1 spans 5 columns for the Geschlecht (gender) radio group: Weiblich, Maennlich, Divers. Row 2 holds address fields: Strasse, PLZ, Ort. Row 3 holds banking fields: IBAN, BIC, Gueltig ab (validFrom). Row 4 spans the width for the RT text area showing server responses. Row 5 has the Anordnen submit button. Female is pre-selected as the default gender via ButtonGroup. The window is 800x650 and exits on close.
business_rules: Gender selection is mutually exclusive: only one of Weiblich (Female), Maennlich (Male), Divers (Diverse) can be active at a time enforced by ButtonGroup
Female (Weiblich) is pre-selected as the default gender when the form first loads
The application window is titled 'Allegro' and displays at 800x650 pixels
The form submit button is labelled 'Anordnen' (German for to order or arrange)
All field labels use German: Vorname, Name, Geburtsdatum, Geschlecht, Strasse, PLZ, Ort, IBAN, BIC, Gueltig ab, RT
The JFrame is configured with EXIT_ON_CLOSE to terminate the application when the window is closed
The form window becomes visible immediately upon PocView construction
technical_libraries: javax.swing (JFrame, JPanel, JTextField, JTextArea, JRadioButton, ButtonGroup, JButton, JLabel, BorderFactory)

method_breakdown.summary: Constructs the view object and immediately invokes initUI() to build and display the Swing form window.
method_breakdown.significance: JFrame window becomes visible immediately upon construction; should ideally be invoked on the Swing Event Dispatch Thread

method_breakdown.summary: Builds the complete 6-column GridBagLayout form with all labels, text fields, gender radio button panel, text area, and submit button; sets the JFrame visible at 800x650.
method_breakdown.significance: Defines the complete visual structure, column layout, field placement, and initial state of the user-facing Allegro data entry form

files.summary: Node.js WebSocket broadcast server on port 1337 that accepts all incoming connections and relays every received UTF-8 text message to all currently connected clients.
files.description: WebsocketServer.js implements a simple publish-subscribe messaging relay using RFC 6455 WebSocket. A plain Node.js HTTP server on port 1337 is wrapped with a WebSocket server. Any client connecting from any origin is unconditionally accepted and added to a global clients array. Any UTF-8 text message received from any client is broadcast synchronously to all clients in the array including the sender. When a client disconnects its entry is spliced out of the clients array immediately.
business_rules: All WebSocket connection requests from any origin are accepted unconditionally without authentication or authorisation
Only UTF-8 text-type messages are processed and relayed; binary-type messages are silently ignored
Every received UTF-8 message is broadcast synchronously to ALL clients currently in the clients array, including the originating sender
Disconnected clients are removed from the clients array immediately on the close event to prevent future message delivery to dead connections
The server listens exclusively on TCP port 1337
Messages are relayed verbatim as-is without any parsing, transformation, or validation
technical_libraries: websocket ^1.0.35 (Node.js RFC 6455 WebSocket server and client implementation)

method_breakdown.summary: Handles new WebSocket connection requests by accepting any origin, adding the connection to the global clients array, logging the event, and registering message and close handlers.
parameters: request (WebSocketRequest): Incoming WebSocket upgrade request with origin and connection details
method_breakdown.significance: Core connection lifecycle entry point; every active client session is registered here

method_breakdown.summary: Receives an inbound message from one client; if UTF-8 type, loops over all entries in clients and calls sendUTF() on each to broadcast the raw JSON string.
parameters: message (IMessage): Incoming message object with 'type' ('utf8' or 'binary') and 'utf8Data' string properties
method_breakdown.significance: Implements the core broadcast relay that enables the Vue.js client to push person and payment data to all Allegro desktop application subscribers

method_breakdown.summary: Removes the closed connection from the clients array using splice(index, 1) to prevent future sendUTF attempts to disconnected clients.
parameters: connection (WebSocketConnection): The closing connection (parameter used only for remoteAddress logging)
method_breakdown.significance: Memory and correctness safeguard preventing broadcast errors to dead WebSocket connections

files.summary: Vue.js 2 application entry point that mounts the root App component onto the #app DOM element.
files.description: main.js is the standard Vue CLI 4 generated entry point. It imports Vue and the App root component, disables the Vue production tip, then bootstraps the Vue 2 application by creating a root Vue instance rendering App and mounting it to the #app DOM element.

files.summary: Root Vue shell component rendering the red 'Search Mock' branded header bar and hosting the Search child component.
files.description: App.vue is the top-level shell component for the Allegro PoC web client. It renders a red (#ff2b06) header bar at 80px height with a logo placeholder and 'Search Mock' branding text at 40pt, then renders the Search component passing 'Search Mock' as the initial content_textarea prop. This component acts purely as a layout container and component registration host.
business_rules: The application header displays 'Search Mock' as the branding text
The Search component receives 'Search Mock' as its initial textarea content via the content_textarea prop

files.summary: Core Vue.js business component implementing client-side person search, two-step result selection, WebSocket connection lifecycle, and data transfer to the Allegro desktop application.
files.description: Search.vue is the primary business component of the Allegro PoC web client. It presents a rich person search form with fields for last name, first name, date of birth, ZIP, city, street, house number, nationality and several disabled read-only fields (customer number, IBAN, BIC, etc.). Searching filters a hardcoded in-memory dataset of 5 persons using OR-combined case-insensitive substring matching. Matching results appear in an interactive table; clicking a row reveals that person's Zahlungsempfaenger (payment recipients) in a second table. A 'Nach ALLEGRO uebernehmen' button sends the selected person with the chosen Zahlungsempfaenger over WebSocket. A Vue watch automatically sends textarea content changes over WebSocket. The WebSocket connection to ws://localhost:1337 is established on component mount.
business_rules: Person search executes against: last name, first name, ZIP code, city, street, and house number form fields
Text field matching is case-insensitive substring search (indexOf >= 0); ZIP code uses exact string equality
Multiple search fields are OR-combined: a person record matches if ANY filled-in search field produces a match
Search executes entirely client-side against a static in-memory mock dataset requiring no backend call

The mock dataset contains exactly 5 hardcoded persons: Hans Mayer (knr:79423984), Linda Reitmayr (knr:67485246), Karl May (knr:13725246), Jens Mueller (knr:41125291), Steffi Ruckmueller (knr:31228193)
Each person has 1-3 Zahlungsempfaenger entries with IBAN, BIC, and valid_from date fields
When transferring to Allegro, the zahlungsempfaenger array is replaced with only the single user-selected Zahlungsempfaenger entry before transmission
Outgoing WebSocket payload structure is always: { target: String, content: Object }
Person data transfer uses target='textfield'; textarea content transfer uses target='textarea'
The textarea content is automatically broadcast over WebSocket on every change via a Vue watch handler without requiring a button click
A deep clone (via JSON.parse(JSON.stringify())) is made of the person record before modifying zahlungsempfaenger to preserve the original search_space data
WebSocket connection to ws://localhost:1337 is established automatically on component mount
Disabled form fields (knr, iban, bic, betriebsbez, rvnr, postfach, bgnr, vorsatzwort, leistung, traegernr) are read-only and excluded from search criteria
technical_libraries: WebSocket (browser-native Web API, no additional library)
files.sub_capability: Client-side Person Search, Selection and Allegro WebSocket Integration

method_breakdown.summary: Creates a new WebSocket instance pointing to ws://localhost:1337 and sets component status to 'connected' when the socket opens.
method_breakdown.significance: Establishes the persistent real-time channel to the Node.js broadcast server; called automatically on mounted hook to ensure channel is ready before any user interaction

method_breakdown.summary: Closes the active WebSocket connection, sets status to 'disconnected', and clears the logs array.
method_breakdown.significance: Clean teardown of the WebSocket channel; not invoked automatically in the current implementation

method_breakdown.summary: Resets search_result to empty, then iterates the search_space array and pushes any person matching the OR-combined search criteria into search_result.
method_breakdown.significance: Core search business logic; the sole method determining which person records appear in the results table; entirely client-side with no server dependency

method_breakdown.summary: Deep-clones the data object via JSON round-trip, replaces zahlungsempfaenger with the single selected entry when target is 'textfield', then sends { target, content } as a JSON string over the WebSocket.
parameters: e (Object|String): Data to transmit - person record Object for target 'textfield' or String content for target 'textarea'
target (String): Channel identifier - 'textfield' routes to Allegro Swing form fields, 'textarea' routes to the text area
method_breakdown.significance: Sole outbound communication method to the entire Allegro ecosystem; deep clone prevents corruption of the original search_space mock data

method_breakdown.summary: Sets selected_result reactive property to the clicked person item from the search results table, triggering conditional rendering of the Zahlungsempfaenger sub-table.
parameters: item (Object): The person record from search_result that the user clicked
method_breakdown.significance: First step of the two-step selection UX: establishes which person's Zahlungsempfaenger entries are displayed in the second table

method_breakdown.summary: Stores the user-selected Zahlungsempfaenger object into zahlungsempfaenger_selected reactive property for later inclusion in the Allegro transfer payload.
parameters: zahlungsempfaenger (Object): The payment recipient record with iban, bic, valid_from fields that was clicked
method_breakdown.significance: Second and final step of the selection UX: the chosen payment recipient replaces the full array in the outgoing WebSocket message

method_breakdown.summary: Vue watch handler that fires on every change to internal_content_textarea and automatically calls sendMessage(val, 'textarea') to broadcast the new content over WebSocket.
parameters: val (String): The new textarea value after the change
method_breakdown.significance: Implements automatic textarea-to-WebSocket synchronisation; every keystroke is broadcast without requiring user to click any button

files.summary: OpenAPI 3.0.1 specification defining the POST /post echo endpoint that accepts and reflects back a PostObject containing all 13 personal data fields.
files.description: api.yml formally specifies the HTTP REST interface for the Allegro PoC. It defines one endpoint: POST /post at server localhost:8080. The PostObject schema contains all 13 personal data fields as string properties. The request body wraps PostObject under a 'user' key. The PostResponseObject response echoes the posted data along with HTTP metadata (args, headers, form, files, origin, url, json). This mirrors the httpbin.org echo API pattern used for integration testing.
business_rules: The API server is located at http://localhost:8080 (matches HttpBinService.URL constant)
The single defined endpoint is POST /post (matches HttpBinService.PATH constant)
The request body is required and wraps the PostObject under a 'user' property

All 13 fields (FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH, STREET, BIC, ORT, ZIP, FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE, IBAN, VALID_FROM, TEXT_AREA) are typed as string in the API schema despite FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE being Boolean in the Java model
A successful submission receives HTTP 200 with a PostResponseObject whose 'json' property echoes the submitted PostObject
The field names in the API schema exactly match the ModelProperties enum constant names used as JSON keys

files.summary: Maven build configuration for the Java Swing application targeting Java 22 with Tyrus WebSocket client and JSON-P dependencies.
files.description: pom.xml defines the Maven build for groupId=websocket_swing, artifactId=websocket_swing, version=0.0.1-SNAPSHOT. The maven-compiler-plugin is configured for Java 22 source and target. Dependencies include the GlassFish Tyrus standalone WebSocket client (present for planned future Java WebSocket integration not yet implemented), the GlassFish WebSocket API, and the GlassFish JSON-P implementation used by HttpBinService for JSON serialisation.

files.summary: Minimal Node.js package manifest declaring 'websocket' ^1.0.35 as the sole runtime dependency for the WebSocket broadcast server.
files.description: The node-server package.json is minimal with only the websocket npm package as a runtime dependency. This package provides the RFC 6455 WebSocket server and client implementation used by WebsocketServer.js to accept connections and broadcast messages.

files.summary: Vue CLI 4 project manifest with Vue 2 as the runtime dependency and Babel, ESLint, and Vue CLI tooling as dev dependencies.
files.description: The node-vue-client package.json defines the Vue.js 2 web application project. It uses Vue CLI 4 (vue-cli-service) for serving, production building, and linting. ESLint is configured inline with vue/essential and eslint:recommended rule sets using babel-eslint as the parser. The browserslist targets modern browser coverage (greater than 1% global usage, last 2 versions). Autoprefixer is configured via postcss plugins.
